mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of GDPC1 growth on 60-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth is followed by negative returns in more than 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate to below 6.5% indicates accelerating labor demand, boosting consumption and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.5%, the proportion of positive 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 55% over any 2-year period, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in housing starts coupled with near-zero interest rates signal a structural housing weakness that outweighs monetary accommodation, implying deteriorating economic momentum and negative equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines 3 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
failure_condition: Instances where the joint condition holds produce 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that are positive in more than 40% of cases, or mean return is not negative with statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5%, rising price pressures force the Fed toward tighter policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In months where CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, the 20-day forward return of the S&P 500 is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return over such periods is not negative at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding factory output and supply-side strength, which lifts corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is less than 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth is followed by negative returns in more than 45% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When growth in retail sales outpaces growth in industrial production, consumer demand is running ahead of supply, signaling robust economic momentum that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, the proportion of positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is less than 55% over any 3-year period, or the average excess return over non-conditional periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% marks the start of a monetary tightening cycle, raising discount rates and reducing equity risk appetite, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.5%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative, or more than 50% of such episodes produce positive returns over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 1000 thousand units signals a strengthening housing sector that boosts construction employment, consumer wealth, and overall economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: In weeks where HOUST exceeds 1000 units, the median 5-day forward return of the S&P 500 is zero or negative, or the fraction of positive returns is below 50% over any 3-year period.
